Patient workflow

  • Admission and continuity stay in the patient context tab.
  • Workup search and modifiers are patient-specific.
  • History and physical exam answers create findings; OpenEvidence prompts are reviewed from the patient tab.

        Local bundle

        Use the phone only for bedside checklist answers and focused notes. Prep, review, and final synthesis stay on desktop.

        Chart intake

        Paste reviewed chart context, de-identify locally, then build the bedside checklist and OpenEvidence tasks from reviewed text.

        Raw chart text is not saved to the vault.

        Local-only input

        Source quality

        Chart note ready Labs included Verify no PHI before copy

        Next steps

        1. Run local structured de-ID.
        2. Review the redacted preview.
        3. Build clinical workup and bedside checklist.

        De-ID review

        Review local redaction before prompts, transfer, or saved summaries. This reduces PHI risk but does not legally certify HIPAA de-identification.

        Reviewed redacted context

        Structured-only local de-ID; raw draft text is not persisted.

        Waiting 
        Run local de-ID to create a reviewed redacted preview.

        PHI review checklist

        • Look for names, MRNs, dates, phone numbers, emails, room numbers, and unique identifiers.
        • Copy or export only content your team has reviewed for identifiers.
        • Copy or export only after checking the preview for PHI.

        Clinical path

        Clinical tools

        Local workup, de-ID, and prompt preparation. No app-server upload by default.

                0 positive 0 review
                Findings stay local on this device until you copy the final update.

                Final update review

                Compile bedside answers, reviewed local notes, and OpenEvidence summaries into a concise update. Copy only after PHI review.

                Update draft

                Keep copied text reviewed and policy-compliant. Verify all recommendations at bedside.

                Review before copy 
                Answer checklist items or import phone findings to build a final update.

                Case status

                Export

                Admit patient to local vault

                Create a patient row for this device. You can paste de-identified context after admission.

                Service settings

                These local preferences shape rounds reports and OpenEvidence prompts for new sessions.

                Primary team prompts use a holistic problem-list view.

                Discharge patient?

                Confirm discharge before removing this patient from the local roster.

                Confirmed discharge removes the patient row, drafts, selected workup, checklist answers, and phone payload from this local vault.

                Rebuild checklist?

                Confirm before replacing the current bedside checklist.

                Rebuilding replaces the checklist rows from the selected workup and clears current bedside answers, item notes, reviewed marks, and phone payloads.

                About privacy

                Local-first PHI risk reduction for reviewed or de-identified prep. It is not a HIPAA certification service, a BAA, policy guidance, or clinical judgment.

                Local vault

                Reviewed checklist state and local patient workflow status can be stored in the opened vault on this device. No cloud upload by default.

                No analytics, telemetry, tracking pixels, or ad scripts.

                PHI review

                Every prompt, transfer bundle, and final update copy action is gated by a PHI review overlay.

                Clinical safety

                Recommendations are educational and operational support only. Verify with the patient, team, and current standards of care.

                Safety note: Do not paste patient identifiers into knowledge tools. This app is for educational and operational support only. Verify all recommendations at bedside.
                All data stays on this device by default. Not a substitute for clinical judgment.

                PHI review before copy

                Check the preview for identifiers before continuing.

                
      
                This is PHI risk reduction, not HIPAA certification. Confirm the content is de-identified and institutionally appropriate before sharing externally.
